

--
-- generated by ai

#include <stdio.h>
#include <sys/stat.h> // Required for struct stat and stat()
#include <string.h>   // Required for strerror()
#include <errno.h>    // Required for errno

int main() {
    // 1. Declare the structure container
    struct stat filestat;

    // 2. Fetch file statistics from a target path
    // Pass the reference using the & operator
    if (stat("/etc/os-release", &filestat) != 0) {
        printf("Error reading file stats: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    // 3. Read data fields directly from your struct variable
    // .st_size holds the total file size in bytes
    printf("File Size: %ld bytes\n", filestat.st_size);

    return 0;
}

-- eoai

-- other options from stat

filestat.st_size: Total size of the file in bytes (type off_t).
filestat.st_mode: File type and permissions bitmask (useful for checking if it is a directory).
filestat.st_mtime: Time of last modification (type time_t).
filestat.st_uid / st_gid: User ID and Group ID of the file owner.

-- stat defined in sys/stat.h header file as below of a mac

struct stat {
	dev_t           st_dev;         /* [XSI] ID of device containing file */
	ino_t           st_ino;         /* [XSI] File serial number */
	mode_t          st_mode;        /* [XSI] Mode of file (see below) */
	nlink_t         st_nlink;       /* [XSI] Number of hard links */
	uid_t           st_uid;         /* [XSI] User ID of the file */
	gid_t           st_gid;         /* [XSI] Group ID of the file */
	dev_t           st_rdev;        /* [XSI] Device ID */
#if !defined(_POSIX_C_SOURCE) || defined(_DARWIN_C_SOURCE)
	struct  timespec st_atimespec;  /* time of last access */
	struct  timespec st_mtimespec;  /* time of last data modification */
	struct  timespec st_ctimespec;  /* time of last status change */
#else
	time_t          st_atime;       /* [XSI] Time of last access */
	long            st_atimensec;   /* nsec of last access */
	time_t          st_mtime;       /* [XSI] Last data modification time */
	long            st_mtimensec;   /* last data modification nsec */
	time_t          st_ctime;       /* [XSI] Time of last status change */
	long            st_ctimensec;   /* nsec of last status change */
#endif
	off_t           st_size;        /* [XSI] file size, in bytes */
	blkcnt_t        st_blocks;      /* [XSI] blocks allocated for file */
	blksize_t       st_blksize;     /* [XSI] optimal blocksize for I/O */
	__uint32_t      st_flags;       /* user defined flags for file */
	__uint32_t      st_gen;         /* file generation number */
	__int32_t       st_lspare;      /* RESERVED: DO NOT USE! */
	__int64_t       st_qspare[2];   /* RESERVED: DO NOT USE! */
};

-- the following function defined in pwd.h. Here it is a pointer of function

struct passwd *getpwuid(uid_t uid);

-- therefore, we need to define the var like below.

#include <sys/stat.h>   // For struct stat
#include <pwd.h>        // For getpwuid and struct passwd

int main(void) {
	struct stat filestat;
	struct passwd *pw = getpwuid(filestat.st_uid);;

	printf("File Owner UID:  %d\n", filestat.st_uid);
    printf("Owner Username:  %s\n", pw->pw_name);
    printf("User Home Dir:   %s\n", pw->pw_dir);
    printf("Default Shell:   %s\n", pw->pw_shell);
}
